newsSinceJun6: Fiscal Q2 2026 results (reported June 5, not reflected in the June 8 verdict): orders surged 41% YoY to $61.6M - strongest intake in years - with Americas +63% and Asia Pacific +66%; sales +17% to $47.6M; gross margin up from 19% to 22% on premium 5-axis/Takumi mix; net loss narrowed to $0.37/sh from $0.62. Balance sheet: ~$50M cash, no debt, $192M equity vs ~$140M market cap. Stock barely reacted (June 9 +0.33%), so the fundamental inflection has not been chased.

sources.label: GlobeNewswire - Hurco Reports Second Quarter Results for Fiscal Year 2026 (Jun 5, 2026)
SEC 8-K exhibit 99.1 - Hurco FY2026 Q2 release
StockTitan - Hurco boosts Q2 2026 sales 17% as orders surge 41%

verdicts.rankRationale: The Q2 print (orders +41% YoY, Americas +63%, margin inflection) is genuinely new evidence of a US machine-tool demand upturn that yesterday's mechanical hold never priced in, and the stock has not re-rated on it - exactly the un-chased fundamental confirmation a strict latent-node alpha list rewards. Net cash of ~$50M covers a third of the $140M cap, improving downside asymmetry.
verdicts.bull: Strongest order intake in years with Americas +63% signals reshoring/specialty-manufacturing capex turning, validating the latent pathway of CNC capacity for hard-to-source AI infrastructure parts; mix shift to 5-axis machines lifts margins while the market still prices a cyclical loser.
verdicts.neutral: Order growth is broad machine-tool cycle recovery, not AI-attributed demand; the company is still loss-making ($0.91/sh loss for six months) and Europe (Germany) remains weak, so the AI-node link stays indirect.
verdicts.bear: If the order surge is tariff-driven pull-forward rather than a durable cycle turn, revenue conversion disappoints and the YTD +30% run unwinds; micro-cap illiquidity makes any exit costly.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q3 FY2026 report (September) confirming order momentum converts to revenue and a return to profitability
verdicts.primaryRisk: Order intake proves a one-quarter tariff/pull-forward spike and losses persist, breaking the recovery narrative
